varieties.name: Hale's Best Jumbo
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Large 4-6 lb fruits, heavily netted rind with light ribs, round to slightly oval shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, sweet orange flesh with excellent flavor, small seed cavity, 12-14% sugar content
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding, vigorous vines, 85-90 days to maturity

varieties.name: Rocky Ford (Eden Gem)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Medium 2-3 lb fruits, heavily netted dark green rind, round shape, 5-5.5 inches diameter
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright green flesh (some strains orange), sweet and spicy flavor, 13-15% sugar content, aromatic
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, compact vines 3-6 feet, 85-90 days to maturity

varieties.name: Hearts of Gold (Hoodoo)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Medium 2-4 lb fruits, heavily netted medium-ribbed rind, round shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, thick, firm, exceptionally sweet and fragrant, small seed cavity
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding, vigorous sprawling vines 6-10 feet, 80-85 days

varieties.name: Minnesota Midget
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Small 1-1.5 lb fruits, 4-6 inches diameter, softball-sized, golden-netted rind
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick bright orange flesh, exceptionally sweet, high sugar content, melt-in-mouth texture
varieties.yield_notes: 6-8 melons per plant, compact vines 3-4 feet, 60-75 days (early)
varieties.availability: Available from seed suppliers worldwide including Australian retailers

varieties.name: Jenny Lind
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Small 1-3 lb fruits, turban-shaped with prominent button on blossom end, heavily netted
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale lime-green flesh, exceptionally sweet and juicy, unique color, excellent flavor

varieties.name: Charentais (French Cantaloupe)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom, true cantaloupe; Small 2-3.5 lb fruits, smooth gray-green rind with subtle ribbing, turns creamy yellow when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep salmon-orange, intensely sweet and aromatic, honey-like fragrance, exceptional gourmet quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, standard vining habit, 75-90 days

varieties.name: Honey Rock (Sugar Rock)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom, AAS Winner 1933; Medium 3-4 lb fruits, coarsely netted rind, round to slightly oval
varieties.flesh_fruit: Salmon-orange, sweet and tender, classic cantaloupe flavor, excellent eating quality
varieties.availability: Available from Australian and international seed suppliers

varieties.name: Iroquois
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Large 5-7 lb fruits, thick coarse netting, gray-green to golden rind, deeply ribbed
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, juicy salmon-orange flesh, very sweet, high sugar content, musky aroma

varieties.name: Edisto 47
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Medium-large 3-5 lb fruits, netted rind, round-oval shape, 6-7 inches diameter
varieties.flesh_fruit: Salmon-orange, juicy, sweet with high sugar content, excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Very vigorous vines with large leaves, high yields, 85 days
varieties.availability: Available from specialty seed suppliers, increasing availability in Australia

varieties.name: Delicious 51
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Large fruits, netted rind, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, juicy orange flesh, excellent flavor, truly delicious

varieties.name: Athena
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; Large 5-6 lb fruits, coarse netting, firm skin, oval shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm salmon-orange flesh, outstanding flavor and aroma, high Brix (13-14%)
varieties.availability: Widely available from commercial seed suppliers in Australia

varieties.name: Aphrodite
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid, Eastern type; Large 6-8 lb fruits, heavy netting, slight sutures, compact vine growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange flesh, sweet honey-like flavor, succulent

varieties.name: Ambrosia
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; Medium-large 4-5 lb fruits, peach-toned netted rind, uniform shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale orange, exceptionally sweet (often >14% Brix), succulent
varieties.availability: Available from major seed companies in Australia

varieties.name: Sarah's Choice
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid, Western type; Small-medium 3-4 lb fruits, heavily netted, attractive oval shape, sutureless
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick orange flesh, sweet, juicy, rich flavor, firm even when fully ripe

varieties.name: Sugar Cube
varieties.traits: F1 Mini hybrid; Small 2 lb personal-sized melons, heavily netted
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet orange flesh, compact size perfect for small families

varieties.name: Sweet Granite
varieties.origin: Recommended by The Diggers Club, Australia
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated; Smaller than commercial types, netted rind
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, performs in cooler climates but excellent in humid subtropical climates
varieties.availability: The Diggers Club, Australian heirloom suppliers

varieties.name: Top Mark
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom, Western type; Medium 3-4 lb fruits, netted rind

varieties.name: Pike
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; Medium 3-4 lb fruits, netted rind

varieties.name: Galia
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Medium 2-3 lb fruits, netted rind, pale green exterior
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright green, aromatic, sweet with hint of spice, unique flavor profile

varieties.name: Halona
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; Medium fruits, netted

varieties.name: Shockwave
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid, long shelf life; Large 4-6 lb fruits, good netting

varieties.name: Arka Jeet
varieties.origin: IIHR (Indian Institute of Horticultural Research), Bangalore, India
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian variety; Round to slightly oval fruits ~1 kg, smooth surface, no netting, no sutures
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamish white flesh, thin (<2 cm), firm, sweet (11-14% TSS)
varieties.availability: Widely available from Indian seed suppliers, tropical monsoon climates agricultural offices, IIHR

varieties.name: Arka Rajhans
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian variety; Round to slightly oval ~1 kg, creamy-white netted rind
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick, firm, sweet (11-14% TSS), uniform quality
varieties.availability: Available through IIHR, Indian agricultural stores, tropical monsoon climates seed suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Sharbati
varieties.origin: IARI (Indian Agricultural Research Institute), New Delhi, India
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian variety; Round fruits, netted skin, compact size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Salmon-orange, firm, thick, moderately sweet (11-12% TSS), small seed cavity
varieties.yield_notes: 15.0 tonnes/ha, early cultivar 85 days, high-yielding hybrid
varieties.availability: Widely available from IARI, Indian seed companies, tropical monsoon climates agricultural stores

varieties.name: Hara Madhu
varieties.origin: PAU (Punjab Agricultural University), India (from Haryana local collection)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian variety; Round, tapering towards stalk-end, ~1 kg, with sutures, green sutures on pale green rind
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light green/yellowish green, juicy, very sweet (13% TSS)

varieties.name: Durgapura Madhu
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian variety; Oblong, 500-600g, pale-green rind, big seed cavity
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light green/yellowish orange, non-juicy, very sweet (13-14% TSS)
varieties.yield_notes: Very early cultivar, confined to specific regions but adaptable

varieties.name: Pusa Madhuras (No. 445)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian selection; Roundish flat ~1 kg or slightly more, pale green skin, sparsely netted with dark green stripes

varieties.name: Pusa Rasraj
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, early-maturing hybrid; Oval fruits, greenish-yellow rind, smooth and sutureless

varieties.name: Punjab Sunehri
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian variety; Moderate surface netting

varieties.name: Punjab Hybrid
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid, Indigenous development; High sugar content, uniform fruit size

varieties.name: Arka Siri
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated selection, Indigenous Indian variety; Elongated globe shape, brownish yellow rind, medium netting
varieties.flesh_fruit: Musky, firm, juicy dark orange flesh, high TSS (12-13%)
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding 25 tonnes/ha vs 17 tonnes/ha for Arka Jeet, 75-80 days

varieties.name: Kashi Madhu
varieties.traits: Hybrid, Indigenous development; Grooved surface with sutures present, dark green sutures on green rind

varieties.name: Hisar Madhur
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, Indigenous Indian variety; Round fruits, brownish-yellow rind with green sutures

varieties.name: MH-51
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid, Indigenous development; Uniform commercial type

varieties.name: MH-27
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; 15th November sowing under low tunnels gives highest yields in trials
varieties.yield_notes: 216.6 q/ha in optimal conditions, 4.0 fruits per plant

varieties.name: MH-10
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; Commercial type, uniform production

varieties.name: GFS Cantaloupe Hybrid
varieties.traits: F1 Cantaloupe segment hybrid; 1.25-1.5 kg, round to oval shape, dense netting, orange flesh
varieties.availability: Green Field Seeds India, widely available in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: GFS Galia Type Hybrid
varieties.traits: F1 Galia type hybrid; 1.5-2 kg, round shape, fine netted fruit skin, green flesh
varieties.yield_notes: Early harvest 36-38 days after flowering, high yields

varieties.name: Edisto 47
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; 3-5 lb fruits, netted, vigorous large-leaved vines

varieties.name: Athena
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; 5-6 lb fruits, firm, heavily netted

varieties.name: Aphrodite
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; 6-8 lb, heavily netted, compact vines

varieties.name: Top Mark
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; 3-4 lb, netted

varieties.name: Hale's Best Jumbo
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; 4-6 lb, heavily netted

varieties.name: PMR-45 (Powdery Mildew Resistant 45)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, historic variety; Commercial shipping type, netted

varieties.name: Trifecta
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Slightly smaller than Edisto, firmer texture, more sutured
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very firm, good sweetness, high quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, slightly earlier than Edisto

varieties.name: Ambrosia
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; 4-5 lb, netted

varieties.name: Sugar Rush
varieties.traits: F1 Hybrid; Does not slip when ripe (must be cut)

varieties.name: Honey Rock (Sugar Rock)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; 3-4 lb, coarsely netted

varieties.name: Hearts of Gold (Hoodoo)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom; 2-4 lb, heavily netted

varieties.name: Charentais (French)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated heirloom, true cantaloupe; 2-3.5 lb, smooth rind
